operation: First, initialize an XY workplane, offset the sketch origin to coordinates (10, 20) and tag this reference position as 'centered'. Draw a 2D profile segment consisting of a 5-unit horizontal line right, a 4-unit long line at 135° polar angle, and a horizontal line ending at x=0, then mirror this segment across the Y-axis to form a closed symmetric base profile, extruding this profile 2 units along the Z-axis to create the lower base. Next, create a new workplane at the tagged 'centered' position, offset its origin by (0, 2), draw an 11x2 unit centered rectangle, extrude this rectangle 2 units along the Z-axis to form the upper raised feature. Finally, apply a 0.5 unit chamfer to all edges parallel to the Z-axis.
geometry: This is a symmetric two-layer solid structure. The lower layer is an elongated flat body with angled end faces and small symmetric notches on both narrow ends. A narrower rectangular raised bar runs along the longitudinal center of the lower base, slightly offset along the base's width axis. All vertical edges of the entire part are beveled with chamfers to remove sharp corners.
function: This is a small alignment insert or mounting bracket, featuring symmetric locating notches for secure assembly positioning and a central raised feature to interface with mating components. Its chamfered edges simplify insertion and eliminate sharp burrs for safe handling.
